title: Pequod Voyage Seed
note: This seed tracks broad route stages for the reader UI. Coordinates are approximate display anchors, not scholarly claims.

waypoints.label: Shore to Water
waypoints.student_note: The first chapter turns a private mood into a movement toward the sea.

waypoints.label: Manhattan Frame
waypoints.student_note: Ishmael begins on shore, using Manhattan and the pull of water to explain why he goes to sea.

waypoints.label: New Bedford
waypoints.student_note: Ishmael enters the whaling world through a cold port town, an inn, a chapel, and Queequeg.

waypoints.label: Spouter-Inn Room
waypoints.student_note: Fear of Queequeg changes into recognition and intimacy.

waypoints.label: New Bedford Chapel
waypoints.student_note: Memorial tablets and the Jonah sermon teach readers how the voyage will think about risk, judgment, and survival.

waypoints.label: Nightgown Room
waypoints.student_note: A quiet room scene turns Ishmael and Queequeg's friendship into warmth, trust, and domestic comedy before the move toward Nantucket.

waypoints.label: Sag Harbor Background
waypoints.student_note: Captain histories and whaling-port reputations widen the shore world before Nantucket.

waypoints.label: Nantucket
waypoints.student_note: Ishmael and Queequeg sign onto the Pequod and leave shore behind.

waypoints.label: Nantucket Contract
waypoints.student_note: The ship, owners, crew, and profit system turn Ishmael's desire for sea into a signed voyage.

waypoints.label: Lee Shore Threshold
waypoints.student_note: Bulkington's brief return marks the moral risk of choosing the open sea.

waypoints.label: Outward Atlantic
waypoints.student_note: The voyage becomes Ahab's hunt, and the first major symbolic and theatrical chapters appear.

waypoints.label: Ahab Appears
waypoints.student_note: The captain's body, silence, and pipe begin turning rumor into command presence.

waypoints.label: Mast-Head Reverie
waypoints.student_note: A lookout chapter becomes a lesson in perception, dreaming, and danger.

waypoints.label: Quarterdeck Oath
waypoints.student_note: Ahab makes the private hunt public and binds the crew to the white whale.

waypoints.label: Forecastle Chorus
waypoints.student_note: The crew's midnight voices widen the book from captain-centered drama to many languages and fears.

waypoints.label: White Whale Theory
waypoints.student_note: Ishmael pauses the action to explain Ahab's obsession and the whale's symbolic terror.

waypoints.label: First Lowering Atlantic
waypoints.student_note: The first lowering turns Ahab's announced hunt into dangerous labor on the water.

waypoints.label: First Gams
waypoints.student_note: Ship encounters begin turning the ocean into a network of rumor, warnings, and competing knowledge.

waypoints.label: Cape and Indian Ocean
waypoints.student_note: The middle voyage spreads into gams, labor chapters, cetology, property law, and repeated warnings.

waypoints.label: Town-Ho Story Atlantic
waypoints.student_note: A nested ship story shows how rumor, mutiny, and prophecy travel between crews.

waypoints.label: Squid and Armada
waypoints.student_note: The voyage moves through false signs, whale schools, and the enormous scale of ocean life.

waypoints.label: Line and Kill
waypoints.student_note: Technical rope-work and whale-killing make danger practical rather than abstract.

waypoints.label: Cutting-In Work
waypoints.student_note: The whale body becomes labor, rope, risk, tools, sharks, and shared dependence.

waypoints.label: Whale Body Reading
waypoints.student_note: The whale body becomes anatomy, measurement, comparison, and interpretive problem.

waypoints.label: Head and Measurement
waypoints.student_note: Ishmael moves from whale heads to measurement, anatomy, and the limits of reading a body.

waypoints.label: Law and Labor Middle Voyage
waypoints.student_note: The middle book turns whale bodies into law, labor, oil, anatomy, fossils, and arguments about extinction.

waypoints.label: Squeeze and Lamp
waypoints.student_note: Labor briefly becomes fellowship and light before the voyage tightens again.

waypoints.label: Try-Works and Oil
waypoints.student_note: Rendering, squeezing, cleaning, and stowing show how whale bodies become cargo and routine.

waypoints.label: Enderby Contrast
waypoints.student_note: Captain Boomer's missing arm throws Ahab's missing leg into sharper relief.

waypoints.label: Pacific Craft
waypoints.student_note: Ahab's body, tools, craft workers, and navigation instruments come under pressure.

waypoints.label: Pacific
waypoints.student_note: Ahab's pressure intensifies as the ship moves toward the final chase.

waypoints.label: Coffin to Life-Buoy
waypoints.student_note: Queequeg's coffin slowly changes meaning until it becomes the object that can save Ishmael.

waypoints.label: Blacksmith Forge
waypoints.student_note: The ship's craft workers help turn Ahab's obsession into tools.

waypoints.label: Storm and Omens
waypoints.student_note: Weather, fire, compass failure, and the log-line make the ship feel trapped in signs.

waypoints.label: Needle and Log
waypoints.student_note: Navigation instruments fail or are remade while Ahab keeps asserting control.

waypoints.label: Rachel Refusal
waypoints.student_note: Ahab refuses a rescue mission, making the moral cost of the chase painfully direct.

waypoints.label: Search and Warning
waypoints.student_note: The Rachel and Delight make rescue and warning visible just before the final pursuit.

waypoints.label: Symphony Threshold
waypoints.student_note: Ahab briefly hears another possible future before returning to the chase.

waypoints.label: Final Chase
waypoints.student_note: The three-day chase destroys the Pequod; the epilogue explains Ishmael's survival.

waypoints.label: Epilogue Survival
waypoints.student_note: The wreck leaves one witness, and the book's survival depends on Ishmael's rescue.

waypoints.label: Final Witness
waypoints.student_note: The wreck resolves the voyage into survival, rescue, and the burden of telling.
